zh.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister於1585年出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。她於1608年10月4日與Deacon James Hurst在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1670年5月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯普萊姆斯享年85歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister于1585年出生在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。她于1608年10月4日与Deacon James Hurst在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1670年5月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼普萊姆斯普萊姆斯享年85岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼諾福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Bannister
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Old French banaste, banastre ‘covering for a cart or wagon; basket’, i.e. a large wicker container. In the 12th century a Norman family of this name had estates in Orne, Normandy, and in England. Ricardus Banastre appears in charters relating to the Earls of Chester c. 1120–29. With what sense the Norman surname was acquired is unknown. It can hardly have been occupational, contrary to Reaney's view that it denoted a basket maker. It is possible that many or even all of the later bearers of the surname were descended from this knightly family. However, several men with this surname in the 14th-century Poll Tax Returns are described as servants or agricultural laborers, while Ricardus Banastr', recorded in 1381 was a butcher. It is conceivable that these men took their name from Middle English banastre, a borrowing of the French word, and that it referred to a basket or hamper they used in their work. Alternatively, they may have belonged to branches of the knightly family that had fallen in the social scale. The term denoting a stair rail is unconnected with this name; it was not used before the 17th century.

variants.name: Banister
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Bannister  .
History: The naturalist John Banister (1650–92) was born in Gloucestershire, England, and came to VA in 1678.

nameMeanings.name: Gertrude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic female personal name, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + þrūþ ‘strength’. The name is not found in England immediately after the Conquest, but only in the later Middle English period. It may have been introduced by migrants from the Low Countries who came to England in connection with the cloth trade, and was certainly in consistent use in some areas throughout the 16th and 17th centuries, although it was not generally popular until the 19th century, when many medieval names were revived. It has now fallen from favour again.

historicEvents.title: The Golden Age
historicEvents.description: "The 17th century era, from roughly 1585 to 1672, was considered to be Amsterdam's ""Golden Age"". The city had formed a worldwide trading network with countries like Africa, Indonesia, Brazil, and North America. The city grew significantly during this time, and was regarded as the wealthiest city in the western world."
Britain waged wars against the Dutch.
"English navigator Henry Hudson, under contract to the United East India Company, sails from Amsterdam and ""discovers""Manhattan Island and the future site of New York."

historicEvents.title: First Dutch Academy is Opened
historicEvents.description: "Samuel Coster, a Dutch playwright, helped establish the ""First Nederduytsche Academy"". It was created with the intent to offer higher education to common citizens and offer higher quality theatre than what was available before. The inauguration took place on September 23, 1617 and included two plays: ""Apollo"" by Suffridus Sixtinus and ""Vande moordt begaen aen Wilhelm van Orangien"" by Gijsbert van Hoghendorp."

historicEvents.title: Athenaeum Illustre of Amsterdam
historicEvents.description: "The Amsterdamse Atheneum was an ""illustrous school"" sponsored by the city, where famous scientists such as Petrus Camper and Caspar Barlaeus taught classes. It was built from the remains of a 1470 chapel (Agnietenklooster). Upon being founded, the City Library was moved to the attics of the building and formed the beginning of what would become the University Library."
Curacao became a colony of the Netherlands.

historicEvents.title: The First Anglo-Dutch War
historicEvents.description: Also referred to as the First Dutch War. This was an entirely sea-based conflict that arose by trade disputes between the United Provinces of the Netherlands against the Commonwealth of England. Ended with the Treaty of Westminster; Holland agreed to respect the Navigation Act and compensate England accordingly.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History of War - First Anglo-Dutch War (1652-1654)

historicEvents.title: Amsterdam City Hall is Opened
historicEvents.description: The City of Amsterdam Town Hall was opened by Mayor Cornelis de Graeff on July 29, 1655. This structure would end up being converted into the Royal Palace of Amsterdam in the future (1806-1810).

en.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister was born in 1585, in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands. She married Deacon James Hurst on 4 October 1608, in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 30 May 1670, in Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 85, and was buried in Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister(은)는 1585년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1608년 10월 4일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에서 Deacon James Hurst(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1670년 5월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 플림우스 플림우스에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 노르폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister nasceu em 1585, em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos. Ela casou-se com Deacon James Hurst em 4 de outubro de 1608, em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister родилась в 1585 г. в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Deacon James Hurst 4 октября 1608 г. в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 30 мая 1670 г. в местности плимоут, плимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности норфолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Gertrude Bannister nació en 1585, en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos. Se casó con Deacon James Hurst el 4 de octubre de 1608, en Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 30 de mayo de 1670, en Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultada en Norfolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
